channel.title: Mystery on Alien Truth Social
channel.description: Recent content in Mystery on Alien Truth Social

item.title: Longdendale lights
item.description:
The Longdendale lights are mystery lights observed in the Longdendale valley and surrounding areas of the Peak District in northern England, with reports dating back centuries and also known as the ‘Devil’s bonfires’. These lights can appear as a single bright light or small clusters of varying colors, sometimes moving rapidly, and have been seen by individuals and groups. Despite the proximity of a busy road, there is no physical evidence like photographs or video of the claimed lights, and explanations have included misidentification of distant lights, aircraft, or celestial bodies. Local legends attribute the lights to witches, spirits, or Roman soldiers’ ghosts, while some suggest misidentification of car or house lights, aircraft, Venus, or gamekeepers’ flashlights.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Longdendale_lights

item.title: Betz mystery sphere
item.description:
The Betz mystery sphere, an approximately 8-inch metal sphere, was discovered by the Betz family in Florida in 1974. The sphere reportedly reacted to sound, rolled on its own, and changed direction, leading Terry Betz to conduct experiments with it. At the time of discovery, the United States Navy suggested it was a ball bearing from a chemical plant’s piping system, and the Miami Herald reported a similar object in Jacksonville was identified as part of a paper mill valve. Later analysis by Skeptoid suggested the sphere was a ball check valve produced by Bell & Howell and proposed that uneven floors could explain its apparent autonomous movement.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Betz_mystery_sphere
